mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is elevated, higher policy rates raise the discount rate on equity cash flows and tighten financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days after the rate level is sustained above the neutral range.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show negative 20-day forward SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC is not below -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income and future demand, which should depress near-term equity returns because earnings expectations are revised down before labor data fully feeds into consumption and profits.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both strengthen, the combination indicates resilient consumer spending and housing-linked demand, supporting earnings breadth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-growth state in RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone in walk-forward tests, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in the top joint quintile.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and operating leverage, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through stronger realized activity expectations and cyclicals leadership.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly changes in INDPRO are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the 1-year rolling hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: If GDPC1 and PAYEMS are both accelerating, broad real activity and payroll income reinforce each other, creating a positive growth surprise that should support S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDP and payroll growth both positive versus prior print
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-growth condition fails to produce above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not positive in rolling out-of-sample regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS rate combined with falling CPIAUCSL inflation implies restrictive real rates remain elevated, which should pressure equity valuations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate and disinflation episodes do not underperform other regimes on 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal is not more negative than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable UNRATE near the current 3.7% level supports wage income and consumption, so the market should drift higher over the next 5 trading days unless labor conditions deteriorate sharply.
failure_condition: Reject if low-unemployment triggers fail to generate positive 5-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if a rising unemployment sub-sample reverses the sign and the pooled signal remains positive.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while HOUST remains weak, production is improving without a matching housing demand impulse, implying a narrow cyclical rebound that should fade and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST below 1500 while INDPRO rises month over month
failure_condition: Reject if the INDPRO-up/HOUST-weak configuration does not precede negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction effect is not negative in rolling out-of-sample tests.
